# SpliceKit

> Final Cut Pro, unlocked. A Command Palette, MCP server, and an open plugin framework to do almost anything.

- [SpliceKit](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/readme.md): SpliceKit
- [Download](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/download.md): You can download the latest SpliceKit on GitHub.
- [System Requirements](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/system-requirements.md): SpliceKit works with Final Cut Pro v12 (lifetime/perpetual) and Final Cut Pro Creator Studio v12 (subscription).
- [Installation](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/installation.md): Make sure you have Final Cut Pro v12 (lifetime/perpetual) and/or Final Cut Pro Creator Studio v12 (subscription) installed.
- [Support](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/support.md): If you run into any bugs, crashes, or you have a feature request, you can post an issue here.
- [Frequently Asked Questions](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/faq.md): SpliceKit lets you control Final Cut Pro with text commands and AI instead of clicking buttons and dragging things around.
- [Troubleshooting](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/troubleshooting.md): Apologies in advance if you have any trouble using SpliceKit.
- [Contribute](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/contribute.md): SpliceKit is open source and anyone can contribute to it.
- [Release Notes](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/release-notes.md): 🎉 Released:
- [Uninstall](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/uninstall.md): To uninstall SpliceKit, simply drag the application to the trash/bin.
- [Credits](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/credits.md): SpliceKit has been created by Brian Elliott Tate.
- [Privacy Policy](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/privacy.md): This is the privacy policy for the SpliceKit, macOS Application that is available on GitHub.

## Features

- [Audio Mixer](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/features/audio-mixer.md): Mix by role, not clip-by-clip. Drop a compressor, EQ, or reverb on your Dialogue bus and every clip tagged Dialogue inherits it — past, present, and future.
- [Command Palette](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/features/command-palette.md): One keystroke to anything.
- [MCP Server](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/features/mcp-server.md): First, create a Python virtual environment for the MCP server and install the mcp package:
- [Native BRAW and VP9 Support](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/features/codec-support.md): Drop Blackmagic RAW (.braw) and VP9/WebM files straight onto your timeline — no transcoding, no wrappers, no third-party toolkit install.
- [Sections](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/features/sections.md): Sections bar for timeline song structure, add default transition to all clips command, fuzzy search stop-word handling.
- [Transcript Editor](https://splicekit.fcp.cafe/features/transcript-editor.md): The Transcript Editor brings Text-Based Video Editing to Final Cut Pro:
